title: The Star and the Three Kings
subtitle: The Epiphany — the Magi follow a new star from the East to the Child

stillness: They who proclaim to us the Sun of Justice come from the East;
with glad tidings they fill the whole world with light.
Let the light of His grace come into the darkness of your conscience.
gospel: On the thirteenth day, the child Jesus showed Himself to the Gentiles.
And behold, Magi came from the East,
from the regions that, toward Jerusalem and Judea, lie to the rising of the sun.
For where the day is born, there too should the beginning of faith proceed,
since faith is the light of souls.
Seeing the new star, by divine inspiration they knew
that this was the star foretold of old through Balaam,
that a star should rise out of Jacob, and a man stand up from Israel.
And at once they set out from their own places
to come and venerate the new-born Child.
behold: Attend to each thing as if you were present.
Behold: these are not workers of dark arts, but the wise — the firstfruits of our faith.
Behold them called Magi, that is, great in wisdom, skilled in the knowledge of the stars.
Behold them called kings, for in that age the wise were accustomed to reign.
Behold the star, unlike every other star:
not set in the firmament, but low in the air, near the earth;
not of heavenly matter, but of a substance that could perish;
no larger, perhaps, than two or three cubits.
Behold it move — not in a circle, but in a straight path, from the rising toward the south.
When the Magi walked, it went; when they stood still, it rested.
It shone at noon, in the full light of the sun,
outshining the very rays of the sun with its own brightness.
It was given to serve Christ alone, and was seen by these three alone.
Behold them seeking in the star Christ, God and man,
whom by divine inspiration they understood the star to signify.
consider: Consider how far the rulers of this age stand from the rulers of that one.
In that golden age it was an office to command, not a kingdom to be grasped;
no one among them could be more powerful unless he were also better.
But now all things are turned against them:
they seek riches, and honors, and delights —
and so perhaps in the world to come they will fare worse than the Gentiles.
Consider that today the Church, in the person of the Magi, was received by Him as His bride;
for she is gathered most of all from the Gentiles.
On the day of His birth He appeared to the Jews in the person of the shepherds,
and of these only a few received the Word of God;
but today He appeared to the Gentiles, who filled the Church.
In the brightness of the star, the grace of God;
in the three Magi, the calling of the Gentiles went before.
Today's feast, therefore, is the feast of the Church, and of the faithful in Christ.
We who withdrew from the homeland of supernal happiness
by the way of darkness and of sin —
let us return to it by the way of truth and of grace, with Him to lead us.
prayer: O good Jesus, born of the Virgin,
who revealed Yourself to the Magi with a star to lead them,
and made them return to their own country by another way:
merciful Jesus, let the light of Your grace shine upon the darkness of my conscience,
and by Your joyful appearing, give me full knowledge of You and of myself,
that within I may see You, within I may find You;
and there offer to Your majesty the myrrh of inward compunction,
and that I, who by the way of darkness and of guilt withdrew
from the homeland of supernal happiness,
may return to it by the way of truth and grace, with You to lead me. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Merito ab Oriente veniunt, qui Solem justitiæ novum nobis ortum annuntiant, lætisque rumoribus totum mundum illuminant.
latinJewel.english: Rightly do they come from the East, who proclaim to us the Sun of Justice newly risen, and with glad tidings fill the whole world with light.
